mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound monetary accommodation. This extreme easing compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-02-15 indicates ZLB regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market tightening and rising consumer income expectations. This strengthens aggregate demand and corporate earnings outlooks, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive SPX returns within 5 trading days in more than 50% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000 thousand units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.3% month-over-month signal synchronized strength in residential and consumer demand. This joint signal indicates late-cycle expansion momentum that drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,065 thousand and RSAFS at 437,006 million as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both HOUST > 1000 and RSAFS MoM > 0.3%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signal supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. This contraction in manufacturing activity predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO MoM declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while federal funds rate remains at zero lower bound signals deflationary pressure and monetary policy impotence. This regime produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates rise and growth expectations weaken.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 234.677 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: deflationary pressure with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL declines MoM and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate combined with retail sales (RSAFS) momentum above 0.5% MoM signals strong aggregate demand expansion. This synchronized demand acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS MoM > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,293.7 billion (Q4 2014) and RSAFS at 437,006 million as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and RSAFS MoM > 0.5%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 5.5% signals tight supply conditions and rising capacity utilization. This supply-constrained expansion regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as pricing power improves.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1878 and UNRATE at 5.5% as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: supply-constrained expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO MoM > 0.4% and UNRATE < 5.5%, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
